title: ～に堪える
titleEn: Used to say that something is 'worth doing' or 'can stand up to' something (the positive counterpart of ～に堪えない).
structure: Verb-dictionary form + に堪える, Noun + に堪える
structureEn: Verb-dictionary form + に堪える, Noun + に堪える
explanation: Used to say that something is 'worth doing' or 'can stand up to' something (the positive counterpart of ～に堪えない). (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～に堪える grammar point indicates that something is good enough or worthy enough to do (read, see, listen to, etc.). Common examples include 読むに堪える, 見るに堪える, 聞くに堪える, etc., meaning 'worth reading/watching/listening to.'

title: ～に耐える
explanation: To endure or withstand something; 'endure', 'withstand', 'tolerate'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～に耐える grammar point is used to express the concept of enduring or withstanding something, often involving a degree of difficulty or hardship. It can be translated as 'endure', 'withstand', or 'tolerate' in English. Here we focus on usage with nouns (e.g., a force or a hardship to endure).

title: ～に至った
titleEn: Expresses the outcome or final conclusion of a process; 'reached the point', 'led to'.
structure: Verb-てform + に至った or Noun / Verb-dictionary + に至る (more general).
structureEn: Verb-てform + に至った or Noun / Verb-dictionary + に至る (more general).
explanation: Expresses the outcome or final conclusion of a process; 'reached the point', 'led to'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～に至った grammar point is used to express the final outcome or conclusion that has been reached as a result of a process or a series of events. It can be translated as 'reached the point', 'led to', or 'resulted in' in English. It often indicates a significant outcome.

title: ～に越したことはない
titleEn: Expresses that something is the best or most desirable course of action; 'nothing better than...'.
structure: Verb (dictionary form) + に越したことはない, い-Adjective + に越したことはない, な-Adjective/Noun + である + に越したことはない
structureEn: Verb (dictionary form) + に越したことはない, い-Adjective + に越したことはない, な-Adjective/Noun + である + に越したことはない
explanation: Expresses that something is the best or most desirable course of action; 'nothing better than...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～に越したことはない grammar point indicates that a certain state or action is the most favorable or ideal. It literally conveys the idea that nothing surpasses the stated condition.

title: ～に難くない
titleEn: Expresses 'not difficult to ~' in a formal/literary sense; e.g., 'not hard to imagine/understand'.
structure: Verb-dictionary form + に難くない (commonly with 想像する, 理解する, 察する, etc.)
structureEn: Verb-dictionary form + に難くない (commonly with 想像する, 理解する, 察する, etc.)
explanation: Expresses 'not difficult to ~' in a formal/literary sense; e.g., 'not hard to imagine/understand'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～に難くない (～にかたくない) grammar is typically used in formal or literary contexts to indicate that something is not difficult. Common expressions include 想像に難くない ('not hard to imagine'), 理解に難くない ('not difficult to understand'), 察するに難くない, and so on.

title: ～のは Noun ぐらいのものだ
titleEn: Conveys that 'only (Noun) is the case' or 'nothing but (Noun)'.
structure: Verb-casual + のは + Noun + ぐらいのものだ, い-Adjective + のは + Noun + ぐらいのものだ, etc.
structureEn: Verb-casual + のは + Noun + ぐらいのものだ, い-Adjective + のは + Noun + ぐらいのものだ, etc.
explanation: Conveys that 'only (Noun) is the case' or 'nothing but (Noun)'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～のは Noun ぐらいのものだ pattern indicates that something is exclusively or solely that particular Noun, conveying the sense of 'That's the only thing...' or 'There's nothing but...' about a situation.

title: ～ば～ものを
titleEn: Expresses regret or a missed opportunity: 'if only', 'had I but...'.
structure: Verb-ば + Verb-past + ものを, い-Adjective + ければ + ものを, な-Adjective/Noun + であれば + ものを, etc.
structureEn: Verb-ば + Verb-past + ものを, い-Adjective + ければ + ものを, な-Adjective/Noun + であれば + ものを, etc.
explanation: Expresses regret or a missed opportunity: 'if only', 'had I but...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ば～ものを grammar point shows regret about what could have been, often translated as 'if only...' or 'it would have been better if...'. It can be used with verbs, adjectives, or nouns, usually in a past or conditional form.

title: ～びた
titleEn: A suffix meaning “looking or seeming like ~” (often appears with nouns/adjectives).
structure: [Noun/Adjective stem] + びた (forms a 連体形 modifier, e.g., 大人びた人 “an adult-like person”)
structureEn: [Noun/Adjective stem] + びた (forms a 連体形 modifier, e.g., 大人びた人 “an adult-like person”)
explanation: A suffix meaning “looking or seeming like ~” (often appears with nouns/adjectives). (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The suffix ～びた attaches to certain words to mean “to have the look/feel of ~.” Common examples include 大人びた (adult-like), 古びた (old/worn), 田舎びた (countrified). It expresses that someone or something appears or has become like that noun/adjective.

title: ～びる
titleEn: A suffix meaning “to appear/to become ~,” often used with certain adjectives or nouns.
structure: Adjective/Noun + びる (to form a verb)
structureEn: Adjective/Noun + びる (to form a verb)
explanation: A suffix meaning “to appear/to become ~,” often used with certain adjectives or nouns. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The suffix ～びる is used to form verbs meaning 'to become like' or 'to seem like.' Typical examples include 大人びる (to become or behave like an adult), 古びる (to become old or look old), 田舎びる (to become rustic/countrified).

title: ～ぶった
titleEn: Often appears as a past form of ～ぶる, meaning 'acted/pretended like ~' (or literally 'hit/bashed', depending on context).
structure: Noun/Adjective + ぶる → ぶった (past tense), e.g., 偉そうぶった, 大人ぶった
structureEn: Noun/Adjective + ぶる → ぶった (past tense), e.g., 偉そうぶった, 大人ぶった
explanation: Often appears as a past form of ～ぶる, meaning 'acted/pretended like ~' (or literally 'hit/bashed', depending on context). (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The verb ～ぶる means 'to act/behave as if one is ~' (e.g., 偉そうにぶる: 'to act all high and mighty'). In its past tense, ～ぶった can describe someone who acted or pretended in a certain way. Be aware that ぶった can also simply be 'hit' (past tense of 打つ/ぶつ) depending on the kanji/spelling, but as a grammar pattern, it’s about 'putting on airs.'
